etymology.description: 𠁳 (pot full of plants, abundance) + 豆 (pot) -> abundance
etymology.interpretation: The character 豐(fēng, 'abundance') visually combines 𠁳, which depicts a pot overflowing with plants, and 豆(dòu, 'pot'), illustrating a vessel full of plenty. This reflects the concept of an overflowing abundance or wealth of resources, emphasizing fullness and prosperity, not specifically related to seedlings.

examples.chinese: 田野里长满了绿油油的丰(豐)。
examples.pinyin: Tiányě lǐ zhǎngmǎnle lǜyóuyóu de fēng.
examples.english: The fields are full of lush green seedlings.

examples.chinese: 这些丰(這些豐)都在农民的精心呵护下成长(成長)。
examples.pinyin: Zhèxiē fēng dōu zài nóngmín de jīngxīn hēhù xià chéngzhǎng.
examples.english: These seedlings thrive under the careful care of the farmers.

examples.chinese: 春雨过后(春雨過後)，丰(豐)迅速地抽出了嫩芽(嫩芽)。
examples.pinyin: Chūnyǔ guòhòu, fēng xùnsù de chōuchūle nènyá.
examples.english: After the spring rain, the seedlings quickly sprouted tender shoots.
